name: Hapa Character Sheet Node
summary: Turn Hapa Second Brain evidence into a professional resume, RPG character sheet, portfolio brochure, and agent-readable dossier.

steps.label: Character Sheet loads skills, evidence, turns, nodes, agents, media, and lineage.
explanation: Second Brain remains the memory engine. It owns source items, exposures, skills, AI turns, node capabilities, agents, and lineage links.
Character Sheet reads those records through a narrow projection adapter so the public interface never has to expose raw personal history by default.

steps.label: The stat engine converts evidence into professional/RPG stats.
explanation: Evidence counts, source diversity, result links, freshness, and review confidence become stat snapshots with formula versions.
Every score is explainable. A stat is not just a number; it is a compact proof trail for what the professional can do.

steps.label: Skill and avatar evidence become quality and experience rankings.
explanation: Skill Quality ranks each skill relative to the current inventory using evidence, sources, artifacts, raw score, capability reach, and node reach.
Avatar Experience ranks avatar-skill pairs from avatar dossier evidence now, and is designed to absorb direct avatar-use telemetry when that source exists.

steps.label: Daily buckets become a day-by-day lore and proof rail.
explanation: The Character Sheet reads Second Brain time_bucket_day metrics and synthesized skill unlock days into timeline.series_by_scale.day.
Humans can switch the Presentation Timeline to Daily, while agents can query the same abstraction through scale=day and inspect daily_summary freshness.

steps.label: Turns, skills, language, lore, agents, and protocols become an appendable personality dossier.
explanation: The Character Profile Mining protocol extracts observation cards from turn patterns, repeated language, skill weights, output history, timeline events, board state, and agent relationships.
The output is a human-readable lore/personality dossier plus an agent persona adapter that preserves evidence status, confidence, redaction, and open questions.

steps.label: Pinned claims become a readable resume and brochure surface.
explanation: The public view shows selected outcomes, skill summaries, portfolio work, and contact/export paths without leaking private raw sources.
The owner can curate language and pin evidence so professional claims stay sharp, truthful, and useful.

steps.label: Skills become explainable cards with source, turn, result, and node links.
explanation: A skill card explains the skill in plain language, shows RPG-style rank and effect text, and links to applied examples.
Agents can ask for the same card as JSON and use the evidence routes to understand where confidence comes from.

steps.label: The agent dossier emits a redaction-safe machine context pack.
explanation: The dossier summarizes professional capabilities, preferred protocols, current board state, and safe next actions.
It gives AI collaborators enough structure to help without dumping every private source or unreviewed inference.

steps.label: Refresh runs rebuild the projection and record last successful freshness.
explanation: The refresh command reads Second Brain, regenerates Character Sheet data, appends refresh events, and reprojects the latest successful timestamp.
Future humans and agents can inspect refresh.last_success before deciding whether skills, metrics, nodes, media, and timeline data need another rebuild.

steps.label: Build progress and review gates are appended to the board event log.
explanation: The board tracks schema, API, CLI, UI, desktop, privacy, export, and verification work as append-only events.
Quest Keeper and Overwatch can project the board for humans and agents while preserving the event log as the coordination source of truth.

steps.label: The protocol becomes a teachable Node Space flow and process card.
explanation: Node Space can animate the route from Second Brain evidence to resume surface, skill codex, agent dossier, and board writeback.
The player learns how Hapa turns memory into a professional identity system while keeping provenance and redaction intact.

card.description: Converts source-backed memory into a showable professional sheet with stats, skills, media, lineage, and agent context.
card.effect: Public viewers get a polished resume; agents get a structured evidence pack; the owner gets curation, redaction, and refresh control.
card.risk: If redaction and review gates are weak, private raw history or inferred claims can leak into public outputs.

record_rule.character_sheet: Owns curated projections, redaction, stat snapshots, exports, and local app state.
record_rule.wiki: Owns canonical protocol explanation after review.
